commit.message:
feat(wisher-details): Add wisher details feature with type-safe routing (#65)

* feat(wisher-details): Add wisher details screen and view model

Implement the wisher details feature with:
- WisherDetailsScreen: Pure UI presentation layer with loading/error states
- WisherDetailsViewModel: Business logic for loading wisher data by ID
- Type-safe navigation: Routes.wisherDetails.buildPath(id: id) helper
- Full MVVM integration with Provider dependency injection

The screen displays a specific wisher's profile when navigated from the
wishers list, with proper loading states and fallback messaging. Uses the
cached wisher repository for data retrieval.

Architecture follows CLAUDE.md standards with abstract contracts and
constructor injection for testability.

Co-authored-by: GitHub Copilot <copilot@github.com>

* fix: standardize navigation pattern to use ViewModel callbacks

- Add tapAddWisher() to HomeViewModel for consistent navigation pattern
- Update HomeScreen to use viewModel.tapAddWisher() instead of direct context.pushNamed()
- Add defensive ArgumentError for missing wisher ID in router.dart instead of silent empty string fallback
- Document navigation pattern in docs/AGENTS.md with correct vs incorrect examples
- Add test for tapAddWisher route name validation

This ensures all navigation is testable, mockable, and follows MVVM principles.

Co-authored-by: GitHub Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

commit.message:
Clarify marketplace skills are always team-scoped

Marketplace skills are generic open-source tools that know nothing about
federation. They're always installed into the team's worktree during
onboarding. Meta-squad skills come exclusively from archetype plugin
installs (global scope, auto-discovered by Copilot).

The distinction is structural, not metadata-based — no audience field
needed. Global install = meta-squad learns it. Worktree install = team.

Co-authored-by: Vladi Lyga <lyvladi@gmail.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix OpenAPI publish job being skipped on manual workflow dispatch (#45)

https://github.com/SubDynamics/event-hub-api/pull/45

The OpenAPI workflow’s `publish-types-package` job could be skipped on
manual runs due to an overly strict branch gate and string-based input
check. This updates job gating so manual dispatch behaves independently
from push-to-main change detection.

- **Condition split by trigger type**
- Kept push behavior scoped to `main` + detected `packages/types/**`
changes.
- Separated manual dispatch logic so it no longer depends on `github.ref
== refs/heads/main`.

- **Typed manual input evaluation**
- Switched from `github.event.inputs.publish == 'true'` to
`inputs.publish` to align with boolean `workflow_dispatch` inputs.

- **Resulting job predicate**
  ```yaml
  if: >
    (github.event_name == 'push' &&
    github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' &&
    needs.generate-and-validate.outputs.types_changed == 'true') ||
    (github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' && inputs.publish)
  ```

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: SubDynamics <23660334+SubDynamics@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(watch): external capability loading + PID tracker (#918, #921) (#922)

* feat(watch): load external WatchCapabilities from .squad/capabilities/ (#918)

Add loadExternalCapabilities() that scans .squad/capabilities/ for .js
files, validates WatchCapability interface conformance, and registers
them in the CapabilityRegistry after built-in capabilities.

- external-loader.ts: loader with graceful error handling per file
- Wire into runWatch() after createDefaultRegistry()
- Export from watch barrel for downstream consumers
- Sample capability template: issue-pipeline.sample.js
- 6 vitest tests covering all edge cases
- Changeset for minor bump

* fix(watch): track child PIDs and cleanup orphans on exit (#921)

Add PidTracker class that tracks child process PIDs spawned during
watch rounds, kills them on exit/crash, and cleans up stale orphans
from previous crashed runs on startup.

- New pid-tracker.ts with cross-platform process tree kill
- Wire into watch/index.ts for startup cleanup and exit handlers
- Wire into execute.ts to track/untrack copilot session PIDs
- Add pidTracker to WatchContext interface
- Export from barrel for external consumers
- Add .watch-pids to .gitignore (runtime state)
- 11 vitest tests covering track/untrack, cleanup, and stale handling

Closes #921

* fix: address Copilot review comments on PR #922

* security: add startup warning banner + block built-in name hijacking

P0 mitigations from Worf's security review:
1. Print security warning listing all external .js files before importing
2. Block external capabilities from overwriting built-in names
3. New test: hijack attempt is rejected, built-in preserved

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <copilot@github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add step segmentation protocol

Implement hierarchical step segmentation as described in Appendix:
- Priority 1: Structural delimiters (\n\n, \n, \\)
- Priority 2: Logic connectors (Therefore, Thus, Hence, So, Consequently)
- Priority 3: Explicit enumerations (Step N:, 1., First,)
- LaTeX math protection (never split inside $...$)
- Max 256 tokens per step to prevent run-on steps

Integrated into:
- MCTS expand: segment multi-step outputs, keep first step only
- format_trajectory: re-segment before numbering for analyst

commit.message:
fix: use npm install instead of npm ci for frontend in CI

The lockfile was generated with npm 11.x but CI runs npm 10.x
(Node 22 default). npm ci strictly validates the lockfile and
fails on optional peer dependency resolution differences between
npm versions (esbuild@0.28.0 via vitest/vite 8).

Using npm install is more lenient and handles cross-version
lockfile compatibility.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix -o table human-friendly output and ensure -o json is always valid/pipeable (#5)

* Fix -o table human-friendly output and ensure -o json is always valid/pipeable

- output.py: rewrite _print_object_as_kv to recursively expand nested
  dicts/lists so no raw Python repr strings appear in table output
- output.py: move sys import to module level; explicit file=sys.stdout
  and flush() for json format to keep stdout clean for piping to jq
- cli.py: add -o/--output option to get_group so users can write
  'mtbsccli get -o json project <id>' (overrides root-level flag)
- test/test_output.py: 17 new tests covering JSON validity, no extra
  lines, no Python repr strings, nested visibility, and flag override

commit.message:
fix(jetson): bind-mount host TensorRT/PyCUDA into Docker container

L4T runtime image does not include TensorRT Python bindings or PyCUDA.
Instead of trying to install them inside the container (which needs
nvcc/CUDA dev headers not in runtime image), bind-mount the host's
pre-installed packages into the container at runtime.

setup_jetson.sh now:
- Auto-detects host site-packages location for tensorrt/pycuda
- Adds -v bind-mount flags to the Docker run command in systemd service
- Falls back to mounting full dist-packages if specific dirs not found

Also:
- Add ENV PATH/LD_LIBRARY_PATH in Dockerfile for CUDA visibility
- Make check_ready() create DB dir if missing (os.makedirs)
- Revert pycuda from requirements.txt (host-provided, not pip)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Schema-first workflow for deliverable archetype

Playbook skill:
- Discovery step: now includes data source identification (APIs, repos,
  wikis, telemetry, org charts) → raw/data-sources.json
- Schema Evolution section: schema emerges from first run, evolves with
  domain understanding, becomes a living contract
- Step 3: observe natural data structure during deep-dives
- Step 5: formalize or validate against deliverable.schema.json
- Schema as Skill: once stabilized, create project-level skill documenting
  schema structure, field meanings, quality expectations, edge cases
- Completion criteria: deliverable.schema.json must exist

aggregate.ts:
- --validate flag: validates each deliverable against JSON Schema
- deliverableSchema config field
- Basic structural validation (type, required fields, array items)
- Per-team validation results with item counts
- Invalid deliverables flagged in manifest (validation_warnings)
- branchPrefix default fixed: scan/ → squad/

repository.name: orion-ui
repository.description: Orion is a retro-styled, cross-platform UI framework written in C. The framework is not embedded or mobile-targeted — it is a desktop GUI framework designed for Linux, macOS, and Windows. - Apply identifier validation in retention purge, export, import, and save_rule
- Validate import path with validate_path_components to prevent directory traversal

Bug fixes:
- Replace silent .filter_map(|r| r.ok()) with .collect::<Result<Vec<_>, _>>()
  in list_snapshots, load_rules, load_org_rules — errors are now propagated
- Reject max_age_days == 0 in save_rule (meaningless/dangerous policy)
- Remove unused dependencies: convergio-security, convergio-telemetry

Replace all hardcoded mock data with real API calls across webApp pages

- Extended api.ts with typed service functions for all endpoints:
  productApi, orderApi, customerApi, expenseApi, paymentApi, reportApi,
  taxApi, kraApi, socialApi, cyberSourceApi (+ all response types)
- DashboardPage: fetch KPIs from profit-summary, recent orders, low-stock
  products, customers count; show loading/empty states; replace revenue
  chart with current-month summary card
- InventoryPage (sub): fetch all products from /v1/products
- OrdersPage (sub + standalone): fetch from /v1/orders
- CustomersPage (sub + standalone): fetch from /v1/customers
- OtherPages: ExpensesPage → /v1/expenses (+ delete), PaymentsPage →
  /v1/payments, ReportsPage → /v1/reports/profit-summary
- SocialPage: InboxTab → /v1/social/inbox + /v1/social/conversations/{id},
  ChannelsTab → /v1/social/channels; message sending uses API
- KraPage: ComplianceTab → /v1/kra/compliance, EtimsTab →
  /v1/kra/etims/history (+ retry), ReturnsTab → /v1/kra/returns (+ generate)
- CyberSourcePage: transactions → /v1/payments/card/transactions,
  saved cards → /v1/payments/card/saved-cards (+ delete)
- TaxPage: TaxRatesTab → /v1/tax/rates (+ toggle, seed, create),
  TaxCalculatorTab uses live rates, RemittancesTab → /v1/tax/remittances,
  SummaryTab computes KPIs from live remittances data
- All pages show loading indicators and appropriate empty state messages

commit.message:
fix(jetson): match Docker container UID with host agrikd user

Root cause: Dockerfile created agrikd with UID 1000, but host's
agrikd user has UID 2010. Mounted volume files (owned by 2010,
mode 644) were inaccessible to container process (UID 1000).

Fix: Pass HOST_UID/HOST_GID as Docker build args so container user
matches host user exactly. Also chmod 666 existing files in logs/
and data/ dirs during setup to handle re-run scenarios.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: audit improvements — latex escape bug, path traversal guard, perf (#5)

- Fix latex_escape() double-escaping bug: backslash replacement
  produced \textbackslash{} whose braces were then re-escaped by
  subsequent replace() calls. Now uses single-pass char iteration.
- Add path traversal protection in PDF download handler: validates
  canonical path is under /tmp/ctt-reports before serving files.
- Replace ReportStatus::Display serde_json roundtrip with direct
  match for zero-allocation formatting.
- Remove redundant report_type re-parse in engine.rs PDF branch
  (already parsed earlier in the function).
- Add 2 new tests: backslash escape correctness, status display.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
